arts.ttl: Reading team news: Chem Campbell among changes and misses return to Charlton Athletic
arts.sum: Reading winger Chem Campbell is likely to have played his final match for the club as he misses out on a return to former club Charlton Athletic. The Wolverhampton Wanderers youngster sees his loan expire on Monday having made 22 appearances and scored five goals. Missing out on a return to the Valley, a ground he called home for a spell last season, he is one of three changes made from the New Year's Day win at Cambridge United. Joel Pereira remains in goal, while a back four of Louie Holzman, Tyler Bindon, Harlee Dean and Abraham Kanu look to keep the Addicks at bay. The midfield trio remains unchanged, with Lewis Wing, Charlie Savage and Harvey Knibbs looking to keep up their fine form.

arts.ttl: Tickets sell out for Q&A session as iconic former Reading boss returns to the town
arts.sum: Tickets for an exclusive Q&A event with legendary Reading FC manager Steve Coppell at the Purple Turtle in April has sold out in a matter of weeks. Coppell guided Reading up into the Premier League in 2006 for the first time in the club's history as his side won the Championship with a record 106 points. The team went on to finish in eighth place in the Premier League the following season - missing out on a European place by a solitary point. The former Manchester United and England winger returns to Reading on Sunday April 27 for a Q&A event, which will see him reminisce about his time at the club. The Q&A starts at 5.30pm and will be followed by audience questions and a chance to meet the former Reading manager.

arts.ttl: Reading sell-out initial allocation for Charlton as thousands descend on capital
arts.sum: Over 2,500 Reading supporters are to be descending on London tomorrow as the initial allocation for Charlton Athletic sells out. Always a popular away day with its deep stand behind the goal, last year's 4-0 defeat at the Valley was the largest away turnout for the Royals last season (2,518). That feat looks set to be repeated this time around as the initial, advanced allocation of 2,510 officially sold out on Thursday. Another 600 tickets have been made available to Royals fans, which can be purchased from the ground at the gate, taking a total possible allocation to over 3,100. Reading last won at the Valley in 2016, a dramatic 4-3 victory for Brian McDermott's side with Deniss Rakels scoring a stoppage-time winner in front of the travelling supporters.

arts.ttl: Reading boss previews 'fresh' Charlton test and speaks of 'greatly experienced' Jones
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt has praised 'greatly experienced' Charlton Athletic boss Nathan Jones ahead of tomorrow's meeting between the pair. Over 2,500 Royals supporters are making the trip to the Valley, looking for a fourth successive win in League One and to remain in the play-off picture. On New Year's Day, the Royals overcame struggling Cambridge United 3-1, despite not playing at their best, and are looking for a first win at the home of the Addicks since 2020. Charlton, sat in 11th, did not play on New Year's Day due to flooding at Crawley Town, giving them an extra few days preparation time in what has been a hectic festive period. Speaking on the test posed by Charlton, Hunt said: "They didn’t play today, their game was postponed, so they’ll be fresh. They have a great manager, greatly experienced from this league and the one above, but we can only go there and put our best foot forward and have a go- 11v11 on the pitch- to see what we can come up with."

arts.ttl: Reading skipper praises experienced defender after first league start in 14 months
arts.sum: Reading captain Lewis Wing has spoken glowingly of teammate Harlee Dean, praising his performance after difficult 12 months. The vastly experienced defender made more 200 appearances each for Brentford and Birmingham City before linking up with the Royals last summer. Despite playing semi-regularly at the start of Ruben Selles' tenure, the 33-year-old was dropped in November 2023 and played just two minutes of League One football in the next six months. Remaining unfancied under Selles until his exit in December, Dean started in the 3-1 victory over Cambridge United on New Year's Day, his first League One start for nearly 18 months. Impressing throughout and being serenaded off the pitch by the sell-out travelling support, Dean could start in Saturday's trip to Charlton Athletic due to injury and illness in the Reading back-line.

arts.ttl: Former Reading striker returns to management with Stoke after Coventry City sacking
arts.sum: Former Reading striker Mark Robins has been appointed new manager at Championship side Stoke City on a long-term contract. The Coventry City legend spent seven years with the Sky Blues and took them from League Two to the brink of the Premier League, losing the Championship play-off final to Luton Town. As well as winning an EFL Trophy, the 55-year-old took the club to the FA Cup semi-final where they narrowly lost to eventual winners, Manchester United. After a poor start to this season, Robins was controversially sacked in November and replaced with Frank Lampard. As a player, Robins failed to score in seven appearances for Reading in 1997 but was a striker across the Premier League and EFL between 1998 and 2004.

arts.ttl: Preview: Charlton Athletic vs. Reading - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Saturday's League One clash between Charlton Athletic and Reading, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.
